channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Protest letters from former Israeli soldiers lay bare profound rifts over the ongoing war
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — When nearly 1,000 Israeli Air Force veterans signed an open letter last week calling for an end to the war in Gaza, the military responded immediately, saying it would dismiss any active reservist who signed the document.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US lawmakers' bipartisan Taiwan visit signals support despite harsh words and tariffs from Trump
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Republican and Democratic lawmakers made their first trip to Taiwan under the new Trump administration a bipartisan one, aiming to show both Taiwan and China that U.S.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, ELLEN KNICKMEYER and SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Career diplomat becomes the face of Trump's 'America First' agenda at the UN
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The highest-ranking U.S. representative now at the United Nations told Congress two years ago that Russia's invasion of Ukraine was “unprovoked" and “unjustified,” urging U.N. members to condemn Moscow’s aggression and demand an end to the war.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump administration seeks explosive expansion of nation's immigration detention system
item.description: JENA, La. (AP) — Amid rural Louisiana's crawfish farms, towering pine trees and cafes serving po'boys, nearly 7,000 people are waiting at immigration detention centers to learn whether they will be expelled from the United States.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida State gunman used deputy mom's former service weapon to kill 2 and wound 6, authorities say
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — The 20-year-old son of a sheriff’s deputy opened fire Thursday at Florida State University with his mother’s former service weapon, killing two men and wounding at least six others, investigators said.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE and DAVID FISCHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: U.S.-born American citizen under ICE hold in Florida after driving from Georgia
item.description: A U.S.-born American citizen was being detained at the request of immigration authorities Thursday despite an advocate showing his U.S. birth certificate in court and a county judge finding no reason for him to be considered an “illegal alien” who illegally entered Florida.
item.dc:creator: Suzanne Gamboa, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lawmakers move to mandate the display of Ten Commandments and ban pride flags in Alabama schools
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — Alabama lawmakers approved a slew of bills on Thursday that would expand the use of Christian texts in public schools and limit protections for LGBTQ+ students, in lockstep with a national conservative agenda that has divided statehouses across the country.
item.dc:creator: SAFIYAH RIDDLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: UnitedHealthcare killing suspect Luigi Mangione indicted as prosecutors push for death penalty
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Luigi Mangione was indicted Thursday on a federal murder charge in the killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, a required step as prosecutors work to make good on the Trump administration’s order to seek the death penalty for what it called a “premeditated, cold-blooded assas
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Video shows 'Call on Jesus' bus explode in ball of flames along Louisiana road
item.description: Authorities are investigating after a commuter bus wrapped with the message "Call on Jesus" caught fire Wednesday, sparking a massive explosion in North Shreveport, Louisiana.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Cheng, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court keeps hold on Trump’s restrictions on birthright citizenship but sets May arguments
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Thursday kept on hold President Donald Trump’s restrictions on birthright citizenship but agreed to hear arguments on the issue in May. Trump’s executive order to end birthright citizenship for the children of people who are in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Train collides with funeral home limo in Tennessee; family of 6 injured
item.description: A funeral limo en route to a funeral — and carrying a family to the funeral — was struck by a train on Thursday in Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: David Royer, KRON4

item.title: Federal judge will wait to rule on an effort to block Trump's executive order on elections
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday said she would wait to decide whether to grant a preliminary injunction to national Democrats and voting rights groups that are challenging President Donald Trump’s recent executive order on elections. U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Idaho judge issues order on Kohberger's bid to have his family guaranteed courtroom seating
item.description: University of Idaho murder trial developments: Judge orders prosecutors to show which Kohberger family members will testify as witnesses, and why, by April 25.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tennessee Black Caucus responds after whips given to state reps
item.description: Tennessee Black Caucus Chair Rep. Jesse Chism released a statement on the "lack of awareness" in the House.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, NewsNation

item.title: Tennessee moms celebrate bill increasing minimum recess time requirements
item.description: “We came in and we just educated. This is the law now and this is what's developmentally appropriate for children and we — apparently — changed a lot of minds,” Morgan Garner with Say Yes to Recess said.
item.dc:creator: Nikki McGee, NewsNation

item.title: Wrong man, wrong bed, wrong treatment: Patient's death prompts medical board to discipline California doctor
item.description: A California physician has been placed on five years’ probation by the state's medical board for administering a course of treatment to a patient that was intended for the patient in an adjacent hospital bed.
item.dc:creator: Robert Price, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Visa cancellations sow panic for international students, with more than 1,000 fearing deportation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — At first, the bar association for immigration attorneys began receiving inquiries from a couple students a day. These were foreigners studying in the U.S., and they'd discovered in early April their legal status had been terminated with little notice.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, MAKIYA SEMINERA and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Two dead and 6 injured in Florida State shooting; gunman is deputy's son, police say
item.description: Authorities identified the suspected shooter as a 20-year-old student who is also the son of a local sheriff's deputy.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, Fox 5

item.title: Former MLS player Aaron Boupendza dies after falling from building in China, soccer officials say
item.description: The Gabonese soccer federation announced Wednesday that Aaron Boupendza, a striker for its men's national team, has died after falling from the 11th floor of a building in China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Obama, Sanders, Ben Carson among most liked politicians, surveys show
item.description: Former President Barack Obama, Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) and former Secretary of Housing and Urban Development Ben Carson are among the most liked politicians, according to new YouGov surveys.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration takes aim at Harvard's international students and tax-exempt status
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration has escalated its ongoing battle with Harvard, threatening to block the university from enrolling international students, who make up 27% of the campus.
item.dc:creator: Annie Ma, Jocelyn Gecker And Collin Binkley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Officials in Puerto Rico demand answers after another blackout leaves 1.4 million without power
item.description: Crews worked early Thursday to restore power to Puerto Rico after a blackout across the entire island that affected the main international airport, several hospitals and hotels filled with Easter vacationers.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Noem announces cancellation of $2.7 million in grants to Harvard
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem on Wednesday announced the cancellation of more than $2.7 million in grants to Harvard University amid tensions between the school and the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: New York family told missing woman was found dead, only to learn she's living in Detroit
item.description: A New York family is navigating an emotional journey after learning their sister, whom officials declared dead last year, is actually alive and living out of state.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Garcia, KRON4

item.title: Frontier Airlines passenger describes terrifying landing after plane’s wheel breaks off
item.description: A Frontier Airlines flight lost a wheel during a terrifying landing at Luis Munoz Marin International Airport in Puerto Rico on Tuesday, a passenger said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 3 dead in multi-boat crash during Major League Fishing tournament in Alabama
item.description: A fatal boat crash during a Major League Fishing tournament in Alabama is currently under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Austin Franklin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Officials demand answers as crews work to restore power after another Puerto Rico blackout
item.description: Crews are working to restore power to Puerto Rico after a blackout across the entire island affected the main airport, several hospitals and hotels filled with Easter vacationers.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Power blackout hits all of Puerto Rico as residents prepare for Easter weekend
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — A power blackout hit all of Puerto Rico on Wednesday as the heavily Catholic U.S. territory prepared to celebrate the Easter weekend.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tennessee’s GOP leads the fight to deny public education to children without documents
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Damian Felipe Jimenez has many dreams about his future — he could be a restaurant owner, a scientist or maybe something else.
item.dc:creator: KIMBERLEE KRUESI and KRISTIN M. HALL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lost toddler found miles from home by dog that guided him to safety, sheriff says
item.description: When an Arizona toddler wandered away from home Monday, a team of deputies, search and rescue volunteers and rangers began their search. In the end, it was a protective pup who track down the 2-year-old.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: California sues to stop Trump from imposing sweeping tariffs
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California Gov. Gavin Newsom sued the Trump administration on Wednesday, challenging the president's authority to impose sweeping tariffs that have set off a global trade war.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Search continues for 20-year-old Chicagoan who went missing when boat capsized in Wisconsin River
item.description: WISCONSIN DELLS, Wis. (WFRV) - The search is still ongoing for the 20-year-old Chicagoan who went missing after a boat capsized in the Wisconsin River on April 3, with one person escaping and another person being found dead a day later. 20-year-old Francesco L.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rosen, WGN 9

item.title: Wife of 'Ghost Adventures' star agrees to plead guilty to conspiracy to murder
item.description: The wife of “Ghost Adventures” star Aaron Goodwin agreed to plead guilty to conspiring to murder her husband as part of a plea deal and could face up to 10 years in prison, documents show.
item.dc:creator: Madina Ansary, KRON4

item.title: Why Utah’s wetlands around the Great Salt Lake may matter more than you think
item.description: Wetlands around the Great Salt Lake are vital to the health of Utah's ecosystem — but did you know that wetlands can also filter out pollutants in the water?
item.dc:creator: Nate Larsen, ABC 4
